title: rothmannia macrophylla
intro: Rothmannia macrophylla, also known as the African dogwood, is a plant species native to tropical Africa. It is known for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties, making it a popular choice for traditional medicine. The plant's health benefits include the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, as well as its use as an antiseptic to treat wounds and skin infections. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or tincture, and it can also be used to make a poultice to apply directly to the affected area.
